Is the game not generous with resources? I mean, is it hard to put high investment on a character?
Yeah I've watched some guides I am in wheel jail maxing that one out currently

not really, the game is very generous, just the way Saya works is quite tricky overall, and a lot of resources are kinda progression-gated (including units and posses, and bazillion silvers to roll, and yes include the free Saya, iirc the free copy will be gone in few days)
there's been quite some new players come because of Saya, hence there's quite a lot of builds. Low investment yes, just-started-poor-man yes, mid investment yes, high investment also yes

like, taking your roster of your acc for example, you can have
- at least poor-man Saya: Saya Lotan and the freebies
- Saya Jenkin: this is a bit tricky, as the other two can be Aigis (welfare, she's great), but the last slot is a bit tight, as one team can be only 2 colors (now the team is red-purple already). The other purple of yours is Liz and she needs some skill to play, while red in lower level is a bit sweaty with HP-loss mechanics in some character (Leigh, Uvhash)
- Saya Tawil: this count as mono red altho it's red-yellow. Tawil is a particular character, if you want to play her you would need to learn everyone in the same color (yes). The skill floor is a bit high but totally runable esp for story.

Speaking of pcaro

PCaro = Purgatory Caro or Divine Caro or Special Caro = a special adjustment buff for Caro realm aka red colors, you have bigger blood pool (furnace), when you pump the furnace to HP you'll also apply Corrosion onto enemy, but also cooldown 3 turns

In end game there are 5 difficulty levels a1 to a5

Clearing a1 with having 5 teams gives you score of 300. That dives around 600-700 silver

If you can clear a2 all, you will get 325 and full silver rewards.

Beyond that only shop currency is given. Which is not as important as silver.

Usually if you use support you cna clear one a4 or a5 stage as well
